commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c8ded9eaaf7a2f46df3d00b7efc794ec8c1be4e9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR wraps memory-lancedb LanceDB table initialization in a three-attempt retry loop and appends a Docker volume hint on `win32` after retries are exhausted.

PR surface: Source +24. Total +24 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: the linked report gives concrete Windows 11 Docker Desktop bind-mount steps and matching LanceDB metadata errors, and current main still initializes the table immediately without retry. I did not run the Windows Docker setup live.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor/human action for real Windows Docker bind-mount proof plus author-side fixes to the retry cleanup and diagnostic trigger; automation cannot provide the external setup proof for them.

target.title: fix(memory-lancedb): add retry mechanism for Windows Docker bind mount sync delays
target.body:
Hey! 👋

This PR adds a retry mechanism to the memory-lancedb plugin to handle Windows Docker bind mount file system sync delays.

## The Problem

When using Docker bind mounts on Windows, the memory-lancedb plugin fails to initialize because of file system sync delays between Windows and Docker containers. This causes:

- Table creation failures
- "Table not found" errors
- Plugin initialization failures

## The Solution

Added a retry mechanism with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 100ms base delay) to handle transient file metadata read errors during table creation. Also added a helpful error message for Windows users suggesting Docker volumes instead of bind mounts.

## What Changed

- Modified `extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts` to include retry logic
- Added Windows-specific error message when all retries are exhausted

## Testing

- All 51 memory-lancedb tests pass
- Real behavior proof verified

Fixes #58139
target.author: mturac
